title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Repository Trace: Reflog Object Recovery
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git reflog object recovery increment in an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
Predict reference, index, and working-tree effects of soft, mixed, hard, merge, and keep resets before execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Reflog Object Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Reflog Object Recovery contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Reflog Object Recovery while producing an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer. Competency probe: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work. A defensible solution applies Reflog Object Recovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-reflog-object-recovery — Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
git status --short
git branch recovery/before-repair
git diff
git reflog -n 4
# Choose restore, revert, or reset only after naming the affected layer.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
